name: Narendra Modi
said: India is the mother of democracy.
did: 2002 Gujarat riots: 1,000+ killed (mostly Muslims) while he was Chief Minister — accused of inaction.
Citizenship Amendment Act — discriminates based on religion (excludes Muslims).
Crackdown on press freedom — India dropped to 161/180 on RSF index.
Kashmir lockdown 2019: internet cut for 18 months, mass detentions.
Pegasus spyware used against journalists, opposition — confirmed by Amnesty/Citizen Lab.
facts: US denied Modi visa 2005-2014 under religious freedom act for Gujarat role.
India press freedom: 161/180 (RSF 2024).
Pegasus spyware on phones of 300+ Indians including journalists — Amnesty/Citizen Lab verified.
Sedition laws used against critics — 13,000+ cases filed.
BBC documentary on Gujarat banned in India — offices raided by tax authorities.

name: Viktor Orbán
did: Dismantled independent judiciary — EU Article 7 proceedings triggered.
Captured 80%+ of Hungarian media through oligarch allies.
EU froze €22B in funds over rule-of-law concerns.
Central European University forced out of the country.
Corruption: Hungary ranked worst in EU on Transparency International CPI.
facts: EU Parliament: Hungary 'no longer a full democracy' (Sept 2022 vote).
Transparency International: Hungary most corrupt EU member state.
OLAF (EU anti-fraud office): systematic misuse of EU funds documented.
Media empire controlled by Orbán allies via KESMA foundation.
Constitutional court packed, independent institutions hollowed.

name: Jair Bolsonaro
did: Amazon deforestation increased 75% during his term (INPE satellite data).
Undermined COVID response — 700,000+ Brazilian deaths (one of highest per capita).
Indicted for falsifying COVID vaccine records.
Jan 8 2023 insurrection attempt — supporters stormed government buildings.
Barred from running for office until 2030 by electoral court.
facts: INPE: Amazon deforestation hit 15-year high under Bolsonaro.
Brazilian Senate COVID CPI: recommended criminal charges for 'crimes against humanity.'
Electoral court: barred from office for 'abuse of power and misuse of media.'
Indicted for falsifying vaccine cards (2024).
Under investigation for role in Jan 8 insurrection attempt.

name: Rodrigo Duterte
said: I will end drugs in six months.
I don't care about human rights.
did: Drug war: 12,000-30,000 extrajudicial killings (UN, Human Rights Watch estimates).
ICC investigation opened for crimes against humanity.
Police incentivized to kill — bounties per body documented.
Jailed opposition senator Leila de Lima for 6+ years on politically motivated charges.
Publicly bragged about personally killing suspects.
facts: ICC authorized investigation into drug war killings (Sept 2021).
UN OHCHR: documented pattern of extrajudicial killings by police.
Human Rights Watch: estimated 12,000+ killed in drug war.
Duterte publicly stated he personally killed people ('I used to do it personally').
De Lima imprisoned 2017-2023 on charges widely condemned as political.
sources: Human Rights Watch — Philippines drug war documentation

name: Nicolás Maduro
said: I continue Chávez's revolution for the people.
did: Economic collapse: GDP fell 75%, worst peacetime contraction in modern history.
7+ million Venezuelans fled the country (UN refugee agency).
Stole 2024 election — refused to publish vote tallies despite international demand.
Political prisoners: 250+ documented by Foro Penal.
UN fact-finding mission: crimes against humanity including torture and extrajudicial killings.
facts: UN Fact-Finding Mission: 'crimes against humanity' including extrajudicial executions (2020, 2022 reports).
ICC investigation opened for crimes against humanity.
UNHCR: 7.7 million Venezuelan refugees/migrants.
Hyperinflation: 1,000,000%+ in 2018.
Foro Penal: 269 political prisoners as of 2024.
2024 election: CNE claimed Maduro won but never published detailed results — widely condemned.
sources: UN Human Rights Council Fact-Finding Mission reports

name: Abdel Fattah el-Sisi
did: Rabaa massacre 2013: at least 817 killed in single day (HRW) — possibly over 1,000.
60,000+ political prisoners (HRW estimate).
Shut down all independent media.
Constitutional changes to allow rule until 2030.
Worst crackdown on civil society in Egyptian history — Amnesty.
facts: Human Rights Watch: Rabaa massacre 'likely crimes against humanity' — 817+ killed Aug 14, 2013.
HRW: estimated 60,000 political prisoners.
RSF: Egypt among top 5 jailers of journalists globally.
Death sentences: mass trials sentencing hundreds at once.
Al Jazeera journalists imprisoned for years.
sources: Human Rights Watch — Rabaa investigation ('All According to Plan')

name: Isaias Afwerki
said: We are building a self-reliant nation.
did: No elections held since 1993 — 30+ years of one-man rule.
Indefinite mandatory national service (effectively forced labor) — UN.
UN Commission: crimes against humanity including enslavement, torture, rape.
Zero press freedom — ranked last or near-last globally every year.
Eritrean forces committed atrocities in Tigray war — documented by UN, Amnesty.
facts: UN Commission of Inquiry (2016): crimes against humanity in 'systematic and widespread manner.'
No independent media — 180/180 dead last on RSF (multiple years).
National service: indefinite conscription described as 'slavery' by UN.
Tigray atrocities: rape, massacres documented by Amnesty, HRW, UN.
sources: UN Commission of Inquiry on Eritrea (A/HRC/32/47)
